summary: High‑energy show featuring hot Jerry solos on Morning Dew and a spot‑on Friend of the Devil with a tasteful Terrapin Station tease; sound is clear enough to be called a solid 4‑star tape.
review: The performance captures an excited crowd and strong playing – Jerry’s scorching solo on “Morning Dew” and the tight, right‑tempo rendition of “Friend of the Devil” (complete with a neat Terrapin Station tease) are repeatedly highlighted. Reviewers note the recording is surprisingly clear for an old tape, earning a solid 4‑star rating despite no explicit source information.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers gave 4–5 stars and praised the musical performance; recording quality is described as good with a consistent 4‑star sound rating, leading to an overall strong positive assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the tape as a '4 star sound' and note it exceeded low‑expectations; no major dropouts or muffling mentioned, but source (soundboard vs. audience) is not specified.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans highlight a tight, well‑paced set with strong energy from start to finish; the band kept the right speed on slower numbers and added a tasteful Terrapin Station tease.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Hot, scorching solos on Morning Dew and excellent tone noted.
